title: cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. horniflorus
intro: Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. horniflorus, also known as the Hungarian mountain broom, is a flowering plant species in the pea family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. horniflorus is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support the immune system and protect against infections. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
